concept.shortdesc: Perform a walk-around inspection before each shift. Check for fluid leaks under the truck. Inspect

conbody.p: Perform a walk-around inspection before each shift. Check for fluid leaks under the truck. Inspect the mast, forks, and carriage for visible damage.
Check engine oil level using the dipstick. The oil level should be between the MIN and MAX marks. Add oil if necessary before starting.
Check coolant level in the overflow reservoir. The level should be between LOW and FULL marks. Never open the radiator cap when the engine is hot.
Check the fuel level. Refuel if necessary. Ensure the fuel cap is secure after refuelling.
Check DEF/Ad-Blue level. Refill if the warning indicator is illuminated. Use only ISO 22241-compliant DEF.
Check hydraulic oil level with the forks lowered and the engine stopped. Oil level should be at the FULL mark on the sight glass.
Page 71 of 500 | CD100S-7 / CD120S-7 / CD140S-7 / CD160S-7 Operation & Maintenance Manual | DL06P Tier-4
Always follow all safety precautions when operating or maintaining this equipment.
Ensure all operators are properly trained and certified before operating the lift truck.
Regular maintenance is essential to safe and efficient operation of the lift truck.
Contact your local CROWN dealer for parts, service, and technical support.
Keep this manual with the lift truck at all times for reference by operators and technicians.
Record all service and maintenance activities in the maintenance logbook.
Only use genuine CROWN replacement parts to maintain warranty and performance.
